intro.title: Level 2: Essentials of Kayak Touring — Assessment

sections.heading: Overview
sections.body: A benchmark of ability at the entry level of the ACA Coastal Kayak development program. The assessment focuses on Core Skills that apply across venues; the three Venue-Specific sets are optional "Developing" skills that show progression toward Level 3.

sections.heading: Both sides
sections.body: Strokes, maneuvers, edging, and support are performed on both sides of the kayak. At Level 2 they need not be equally effective to meet the standard — some decrease in effectiveness or efficiency between sides is allowed.

sections.heading: Venue & conditions
sections.body: Calm, protected water with constant access to a safe landing, within 0.5 nm of shore.
items: Waves / shore break: no higher than 1 ft (0.3 m)

sections.heading: Assessor
sections.body: Offered by Level 2 EKT (or higher) ACA Instructors, Instructor Trainers, or Instructor Trainer Educators.

sections.heading: Determining success
sections.body: The paddler must meet or exceed the standard for every Assessed Skill. Any Below means an action plan outlining practice areas and a recommended return date. The CMS Paddler Evaluation records only Meets or Below, with detail in the comment fields.

sections.heading: Momentary conditions
sections.body: If a momentary increase beyond the parameters prevents a skill, it cannot be scored Below — give the paddler another opportunity once conditions return to range.

skills.name: Secure kayak for transport
skills.standard: Make sure the kayak is securely fastened to the car or racks, using proper tie downs, straps, or knots.

skills.name: Lift & carry
skills.standard: Use proper techniques to safely lift and carry the kayak on shore.

skills.name: Weather & hazards
skills.standard: Obtain (recite) weather conditions, forecasts, and evaluate hazards to navigation and other environmental factors.

skills.name: Life jacket
skills.standard: Put on a life jacket appropriate for the operator's size, kayak, and activity; confirm all others in the group are properly wearing their life jacket.

skills.name: Inspect kayak & safety equipment
skills.standard: Inspect the kayak and all safety equipment to meet state, federal, and local requirements for the vessel and activity.

skills.name: Safe boating practices
skills.standard: Recognize, describe and follow safe boating practices (behavior, substance abuse, on-water and land etiquette, Leave No Trace ethics).

skills.name: Prepare for departure
skills.standard: Prepare kayak for departure, stowing gear securely, ensuring it is balanced.

skills.name: Float plan knowledge
skills.standard: Demonstrate knowledge of a float plan (components and filing).

skills.name: Paddle orientation & grip
skills.standard: Hold the paddle in correct orientation and grip for effective paddling.

skills.competency: The paddler demonstrates their preparedness to prevent incidents from occurring. Can manage an incident when it does happen by having the appropriate equipment for the environment and the knowledge to use it. Has prepared and shared an appropriate float plan and knows who to contact, as well as the proper way to do so in the case of an incident that requires outside assistance.
skills.name: Securing Kayak to Rack
skills.standard: Properly secures their kayak to rack when storing or transporting.
skills.exceedsStandard: Can assist others with properly securing their kayaks for storage or transport.
skills.belowStandard: Does not properly secure their kayak.
skills.l1Standard: Make sure the kayak is securely fastened to the car or racks, using proper tie downs, straps, or knots.

skills.competency: The paddler demonstrates their preparedness to prevent incidents from occurring. Can manage an incident when it does happen by having the appropriate equipment for the environment and the knowledge to use it. Has prepared and shared an appropriate float plan and knows who to contact, as well as the proper way to do so in the case of an incident that requires outside assistance.
skills.name: Lifting and Carrying
skills.standard: Lifts and carries their kayak safely and efficiently.
skills.exceedsStandard: Demonstrates multiple ways to carry their kayak safely. Maintains an awareness of the environment and potential hazards in which they are carrying their kayak. Uses a 2-person carry when available.
skills.belowStandard: Lifts in a less-than-safe manner or causes others to avoid them while carrying their kayak. (Note: An inability to carry one’s kayak does not count against an otherwise passing assessment.
skills.l1Standard: Use proper techniques to safely lift and carry the kayak on shore.

skills.competency: The paddler demonstrates their preparedness to prevent incidents from occurring. Can manage an incident when it does happen by having the appropriate equipment for the environment and the knowledge to use it. Has prepared and shared an appropriate float plan and knows who to contact, as well as the proper way to do so in the case of an incident that requires outside assistance.
skills.name: Launching and Landing
skills.standard: Launches and lands at a dock or shore safely.
skills.exceedsStandard: Launches and lands forwards and backwards without loss of control. Is aware of the environment in which they are launching and landing, including the physical nature of the environment, as well as the presence of other users and wildlife, and exercises due regard so as not to present a hazard to them or disrupt their activity.
skills.belowStandard: Capsizes while launching or landing or causes other users to avoid them while doing so.
skills.l1Standard: Enter and launch from shore in a controlled manner, checking for clear departure.

skills.competency: The paddler demonstrates their preparedness to prevent incidents from occurring. Can manage an incident when it does happen by having the appropriate equipment for the environment and the knowledge to use it. Has prepared and shared an appropriate float plan and knows who to contact, as well as the proper way to do so in the case of an incident that requires outside assistance.
skills.name: Signaling
skills.standard: Carries and demonstrates proper use of an audible signaling device.
skills.exceedsStandard: Has more than one audible signaling device.
skills.belowStandard: Does not have an audible signaling device.
skills.l1Standard: Recognize and appropriately use communication (paddle and whistle) signals.

skills.competency: The paddler demonstrates their preparedness to prevent incidents from occurring. Can manage an incident when it does happen by having the appropriate equipment for the environment and the knowledge to use it. Has prepared and shared an appropriate float plan and knows who to contact, as well as the proper way to do so in the case of an incident that requires outside assistance.
skills.name: Communication Device
skills.standard: Has a reliable communication device readily available and knows proper device usage.
skills.exceedsStandard: Has multiple communication devices and knows proper usage of each.
skills.belowStandard: Does not have a communication device or how to use the device they have.

skills.competency: The paddler demonstrates their preparedness to prevent incidents from occurring. Can manage an incident when it does happen by having the appropriate equipment for the environment and the knowledge to use it. Has prepared and shared an appropriate float plan and knows who to contact, as well as the proper way to do so in the case of an incident that requires outside assistance.
skills.name: Float Plan
skills.standard: Files a complete and accurate float plan with an appropriate point of contact.
skills.exceedsStandard: Understands float plans as a trip planning tool and a “safety net.”
skills.belowStandard: Does not submit a float plan or does not include appropriate information for their outing.
skills.l1Standard: Demonstrate knowledge of a float plan (components and filing).

skills.name: Launch from shore
skills.standard: Enter and launch from shore in a controlled manner, checking for clear departure.

skills.name: Forward, straight line
skills.standard: Propel the kayak forward in a straight line 15-20 kayak lengths.

skills.name: Stop
skills.standard: Stop the kayak within two kayak lengths.

skills.name: Reverse
skills.standard: Move the kayak backwards 3-4 kayak lengths.

skills.name: Draw sideways
skills.standard: Move the kayak sideways 10 feet (3 meters) to each side.

skills.name: Turn from stationary
skills.standard: Turn the kayak from a stationary position 180° to the right and left.

skills.name: Turn on the move
skills.standard: Turn the kayak while maintaining forward motion 90° to the right and left.

skills.name: Figure-8 course
skills.standard: Propel the kayak in a figure-of-8 course around markers 3-4 kayak lengths apart.

skills.name: Tripping
skills.standard: Experience paddling a day trip of at least short duration (1 hour) and distance (1 mile / 1.6 km).

skills.name: Land & exit
skills.standard: Arrive at destination point, checking for clear approach, and exit the kayak in a controlled and safe manner.

skills.name: Secure when unattended
skills.standard: Secure the kayak and equipment before leaving unattended, with attention to environment and conditions.

skills.competency: The paddler displays connectivity between the paddle and kayak through safe and effective body movement. And a clean catch, power, and recovery of the blade at appropriate positions with correct blade orientation.
skills.name: Forward Paddling
skills.standard: Moves the kayak 200 yards/meters in a reasonably straight line.
skills.exceedsStandard: Maintains a reasonably straight course with minimal deviation. There is evidence of core engagement. The paddler can vary their speed and accelerate smoothly.
skills.belowStandard: Kayak wobbles or yaws significantly, and the paddler cannot maintain directional control.
skills.l1Standard: Propel the kayak forward in a straight line 15-20 kayak lengths.

skills.competency: The paddler displays connectivity between the paddle and kayak through safe and effective body movement. And a clean catch, power, and recovery of the blade at appropriate positions with correct blade orientation.
skills.name: Reverse Paddling
skills.standard: Moves the kayak 50 yards/meters with reasonable control.
skills.exceedsStandard: There is evidence of core engagement. Can paddle straight backwards at different speeds. Uses corrective strokes to maintain course without stopping or using forward strokes.
skills.belowStandard: Kayak shows significant wobble or yaw and does not go backwards reasonably straight.
skills.l1Standard: Move the kayak backwards 3-4 kayak lengths.

skills.competency: The paddler displays connectivity between the paddle and kayak through safe and effective body movement. And a clean catch, power, and recovery of the blade at appropriate positions with correct blade orientation.
skills.name: Stopping
skills.standard: Stops the kayak from a good speed (2 to 2.5 knots), in forward and reverse.
skills.exceedsStandard: Stops from a good speed within one kayak length (5 yards/meters) while demonstrating good use of core muscles.
skills.belowStandard: The kayak takes more than two kayak lengths (10 yards/meters) to stop, or the kayak capsizes when attempting to stop or displays unsafe body positioning.
skills.l1Standard: Stop the kayak within two kayak lengths.

skills.competency: The paddler displays connectivity between the paddle and kayak through safe and effective body movement. And a clean catch, power, and recovery of the blade at appropriate positions with correct blade orientation.
skills.name: Forward & Reverse Sweep
skills.standard: Turns the kayak smoothly while moving forward or in reverse without losing momentum.
skills.exceedsStandard: The paddler executes smooth sweep strokes using a consistent edge, extension of the blade, and core muscle involvement.
skills.belowStandard: Kayak turns minimally, arms are used to apply force, and no edging is evident.
skills.l1Standard: Turn the kayak from a stationary position 180° to the right and left.

skills.competency: The paddler can combine sweeps, forward, reverse, stopping, and stern rudder strokes to maneuver their kayak effectively.
skills.name: Rotate in place 360°
skills.standard: Spins the kayak in place with minimal forward or reverse movement.
skills.exceedsStandard: The paddler combines forward and reverse sweep strokes smoothly using core muscles and a solid, stable, and appropriate edge. The paddler uses a reasonable number of strokes and minimal forward or reverse movement.
skills.belowStandard: The kayak has significant forward and reverse momentum and does not use appropriate edging.
skills.l1Standard: Turn the kayak from a stationary position 180° to the right and left.

skills.competency: The paddler can combine sweeps, forward, reverse, stopping, and stern rudder strokes to maneuver their kayak effectively.
skills.name: Turning on the Move
skills.standard: Maneuvers the kayak smoothly around obstacles.
skills.exceedsStandard: Paddler uses a combination of strokes with core muscle engagement and appropriate stable edging of the kayak to turn around obstacles requiring a turn of 30 degrees or more.
skills.l1Standard: Turn the kayak while maintaining forward motion 90° to the right and left.

skills.competency: The paddler can combine sweeps, forward, reverse, stopping, and stern rudder strokes to maneuver their kayak effectively.
skills.name: Draw Sideways
skills.standard: Moves the kayak sideways 10 feet (3.3 meters).
skills.exceedsStandard: The kayak moves straight sideways, the paddle shaft is as vertical as possible, and recoveries are clean. Can move the kayak sideways while on the move (hanging draw).
skills.l1Standard: Move the kayak sideways 10 feet (3 meters) to each side.

skills.competency: The paddler can combine sweeps, forward, reverse, stopping, and stern rudder strokes to maneuver their kayak effectively.
skills.name: Ruddering (Stern)
skills.standard: Holds the kayak on a straight course or turns toward the paddle side to correct a course.
skills.exceedsStandard: The paddle is used as a rudder to keep the kayak on a straight course for at least two kayak lengths or to adjust the course as needed. There is evidence of the use of core muscles. The paddler can turn the kayak towards or away from the paddle.
skills.belowStandard: Kayak does not turn to the onside (towards the paddle). Or there is no evidence of the use of core muscles.

skills.competency: The paddler can combine sweeps, forward, reverse, stopping, and stern rudder strokes to maneuver their kayak effectively.
skills.name: Stern Rudder (away side)
skills.standard: Turns away from the paddle side while stern ruddering.

skills.competency: The paddler can combine sweeps, forward, reverse, stopping, and stern rudder strokes to maneuver their kayak effectively.
skills.name: Linked Maneuvers
skills.standard: Paddles a course requiring various turning maneuvers safely, effectively, and efficiently.

skills.competency: The paddler applies consistent and purposeful edging to make turning strokes more effective and efficient. They can stabilize their kayak, with and without momentum, and prevent capsizing.
skills.name: Low Brace Recovery
skills.standard: Prevents capsize when off balance.
skills.exceedsStandard: Paddler can recover after being off balance.
skills.belowStandard: The paddler does not go off balance, or their attempt to recover results in a capsize.

skills.competency: The paddler applies consistent and purposeful edging to make turning strokes more effective and efficient. They can stabilize their kayak, with and without momentum, and prevent capsizing.
skills.name: Low Brace Turn
skills.standard: Makes a turn of at least 90 degrees.
skills.exceedsStandard: Kayak turns at least 90 degrees. The maneuver is initiated with an effective forward sweep, stable and effective edge, and stable low brace, which is required to prevent capsizing. The paddler can vary the arc of the turn and is equally competent on both sides.
skills.belowStandard: Paddler capsizes, kayak turns less than 90 degrees, or significant wobble is evident.

skills.competency: The paddler applies consistent and purposeful edging to make turning strokes more effective and efficient. They can stabilize their kayak, with and without momentum, and prevent capsizing.
skills.name: Edge Control
skills.standard: Edges with purpose and consistency during strokes and maneuvers.
skills.exceedsStandard: The paddler uses appropriate edging to facilitate turns and maneuvers. Edging is stable and consistent.
skills.belowStandard: Edging is inconsistent or absent.

skills.competency: The paddler applies consistent and purposeful edging to make turning strokes more effective and efficient. They can stabilize their kayak, with and without momentum, and prevent capsizing.
skills.name: Turn Control
skills.standard: Able to vary the radius of their turns.

skills.name: Equipment knowledge
skills.standard: Equipment: working knowledge of paddling equipment and terminology.

skills.name: Safety & hazard planning
skills.standard: Safety: understand the dangers of kayaking and how to plan an appropriate course to avoid potential hazardous situations.

skills.name: Cold water / thermal
skills.standard: Cold water shock, hypothermia and hyperthermia; prevention and treatment.

skills.name: Communication signals
skills.standard: Recognize and appropriately use communication (paddle and whistle) signals.

skills.name: Navigational rules
skills.standard: Follow basic navigational rules for inland waterways.

skills.name: Group awareness
skills.standard: Display awareness of the group and employ effective on-water management techniques.

skills.competency: The paddler has a heightened awareness of the environment. They adapt plans accordingly and clearly communicate with the group to make necessary changes. They are properly equipped for the conditions and use equipment appropriately to maintain personal and group safety and comfort.
skills.name: Awareness
skills.standard: Demonstrates awareness of self, group members, and environment.
skills.exceedsStandard: Helps others to stay aware of the environment and “connected” with the group.
skills.belowStandard: Is disconnected from the group and is unaware of their abilities relative to the conditions.
skills.l1Standard: Display awareness of the group and employ effective on-water management techniques.

skills.competency: The paddler has a heightened awareness of the environment. They adapt plans accordingly and clearly communicate with the group to make necessary changes. They are properly equipped for the conditions and use equipment appropriately to maintain personal and group safety and comfort.
skills.name: Leave No Trace
skills.standard: Interacts appropriately with the ecosystem and wildlife.
skills.exceedsStandard: Unconsciously models best practices of LNT Principles and NOAA Marine Wildlife Viewing Guidelines and respectfully makes group members aware of best practices.
skills.belowStandard: Is unaware of, or does not adhere to, LNT Principles and NOAA Marine Wildlife Viewing Guidelines

skills.competency: The paddler has a heightened awareness of the environment. They adapt plans accordingly and clearly communicate with the group to make necessary changes. They are properly equipped for the conditions and use equipment appropriately to maintain personal and group safety and comfort.
skills.name: Kayak
skills.standard: Working knowledge of types of kayaks suitable for paddling in Level 2 conditions.
skills.exceedsStandard: Has a thorough understanding of the advantages and disadvantages of different styles of kayaks in different environments.
skills.belowStandard: The advantages and disadvantages of different kayaks for paddling in Level 2 venues are unclear.

skills.competency: The paddler has a heightened awareness of the environment. They adapt plans accordingly and clearly communicate with the group to make necessary changes. They are properly equipped for the conditions and use equipment appropriately to maintain personal and group safety and comfort.
skills.name: Equipment
skills.standard: Working knowledge of paddling equipment.
skills.exceedsStandard: Has a solid rationale for everything they carry or do not carry.
skills.belowStandard: Is unclear what gear they should carry, why they carry what they have, or how to use the equipment they have chosen to carry.
skills.l1Standard: Equipment: working knowledge of paddling equipment and terminology.

skills.competency: The paddler has a heightened awareness of the environment. They adapt plans accordingly and clearly communicate with the group to make necessary changes. They are properly equipped for the conditions and use equipment appropriately to maintain personal and group safety and comfort.
skills.name: Cold-Water Shock
skills.standard: Understands cold-water shock and the sudden onset of issues associated with immersion in cold water.
skills.exceedsStandard: Has a solid understanding of cold-water shock and its prevention, recognition of, and proper treatment/response, and knows how to mitigate it.
skills.belowStandard: Is unclear what cold-water shock is or does not know how to prevent it, recognize it, or what the proper treatment/response is to mitigate it.
skills.l1Standard: Cold water shock, hypothermia and hyperthermia; prevention and treatment.

skills.competency: The paddler has a heightened awareness of the environment. They adapt plans accordingly and clearly communicate with the group to make necessary changes. They are properly equipped for the conditions and use equipment appropriately to maintain personal and group safety and comfort.
skills.name: Hypothermia & Hyperthermia
skills.standard: Working knowledge of the issues associated with both, including prevention, recognition, and basic treatment.
skills.exceedsStandard: Has a solid understanding of hypothermia and hyperthermia and how to mitigate both with prevention, recognition, and the proper treatment/response.
skills.belowStandard: Is unclear what hypothermia and hyperthermia are, or does not know how to prevent, recognize, or what the proper treatment/response is.
skills.l1Standard: Cold water shock, hypothermia and hyperthermia; prevention and treatment.

skills.competency: The paddler successfully plans and executes routes in Level 2 conditions and venues, considering basic safety and location-specific issues. Planning includes consideration of weather forecasts, currents, the makeup of the paddling group, and group members’ equipment.
skills.name: Forecasts
skills.standard: Gathers, understands, and utilizes appropriate weather and marine forecasts.
skills.exceedsStandard: Comes prepared with all pertinent forecast data and can assist others in finding data for the area.
skills.belowStandard: Has not gathered weather forecasts or is unaware of weather conditions on the day of the assessment.
skills.l1Standard: Obtain (recite) weather conditions, forecasts, and evaluate hazards to navigation and other environmental factors.

skills.competency: The paddler successfully plans and executes routes in Level 2 conditions and venues, considering basic safety and location-specific issues. Planning includes consideration of weather forecasts, currents, the makeup of the paddling group, and group members’ equipment.
skills.name: Chart Use
skills.standard: Demonstrates use of charts, aerial images, weather and marine forecasts, and knowledge of their paddling group when route planning.
skills.exceedsStandard: Demonstrates a high level of understanding of these materials when trip planning to make route, launching and landing, emergency evacuation, rest stop, and shuttle decisions.
skills.belowStandard: Is unsuccessful in completing the tabletop/“decktop” navigation exercise.

skills.competency: The paddler successfully plans and executes routes in Level 2 conditions and venues, considering basic safety and location-specific issues. Planning includes consideration of weather forecasts, currents, the makeup of the paddling group, and group members’ equipment.
skills.name: Trip Planning — wind/waves/currents
skills.standard: Incorporates wind, waves, and currents into route plan and trip choices.
skills.exceedsStandard: Gathers forecasts and understands how wave characteristics (size, direction) can be influenced by wind conditions. Applies a more in-depth understanding of wind forecasts (strength, trend, fetch, duration, and direction) and can infer the effect on the group’s plan from relief maps and headlands.
skills.belowStandard: Unable to gather and use forecast data on wind, wave, and swell conditions to determine if the route is feasible and fails to adjust the trip plan to deal with adverse conditions when necessary.

skills.competency: The paddler successfully plans and executes routes in Level 2 conditions and venues, considering basic safety and location-specific issues. Planning includes consideration of weather forecasts, currents, the makeup of the paddling group, and group members’ equipment.
skills.name: Trip Planning — direction of flow
skills.standard: Incorporates direction of flow into route plan and trip choices.
skills.exceedsStandard: Gathers river or tidal current data and understands how it will affect the progress on a trip. Applies a more in-depth understanding of the current and can infer from maps and charts the current's effect on the group’s plan. Uses this information to choose suitable launching and landing locations and timing to present adequate water depth. Able to explain concepts to others clearly and understandably.
skills.belowStandard: Unable to gather and use current data to determine if a route is feasible or fails to adjust the trip plan to deal with adverse conditions when necessary.

skills.competency: The paddler successfully plans and executes routes in Level 2 conditions and venues, considering basic safety and location-specific issues. Planning includes consideration of weather forecasts, currents, the makeup of the paddling group, and group members’ equipment.
skills.name: Route Finding
skills.standard: Uses appropriate map or chart to know where they are and where they are going.
skills.exceedsStandard: Can assist others in identifying their location on a map or chart. They help keep the group on their intended route.
skills.belowStandard: Is unsure where they are and where they are going on a map or chart.

skills.competency: The paddler successfully plans and executes routes in Level 2 conditions and venues, considering basic safety and location-specific issues. Planning includes consideration of weather forecasts, currents, the makeup of the paddling group, and group members’ equipment.
skills.name: Compass Use
skills.standard: Demonstrates using handheld and deck-mounted compasses to take bearings.
skills.exceedsStandard: Demonstrates taking bearings and using this information to determine their location on a chart. The paddler easily maintains a course using a compass.
skills.belowStandard: Cannot discuss parts and functions of a compass and its use or cannot use a compass.

skills.competency: The paddler successfully plans and executes routes in Level 2 conditions and venues, considering basic safety and location-specific issues. Planning includes consideration of weather forecasts, currents, the makeup of the paddling group, and group members’ equipment.
skills.name: Nautical Rules of the Road
skills.standard: Understands the Nautical Rules of the Road and demonstrates safe paddling in relation to boat traffic.
skills.exceedsStandard: Understands a wide variety of nautical rules of the road. Leads a group safely through an area of active boating traffic, positioned appropriately to aids to navigation. Effectively organizes the group when crossing shipping channels.
skills.belowStandard: Knows some rules or no rules. Paddles a haphazard route through areas of boating traffic and aids to navigation.
skills.l1Standard: Follow basic navigational rules for inland waterways.

skills.competency: The paddler successfully plans and executes routes in Level 2 conditions and venues, considering basic safety and location-specific issues. Planning includes consideration of weather forecasts, currents, the makeup of the paddling group, and group members’ equipment.
skills.name: Tide & Tidal Drift
skills.standard: Demonstrates ability to calculate tide and tidal drift stages and incorporate results into a trip plan.

skills.name: Principles of rescue
skills.standard: Demonstrate Principles of Rescue: safe rescue strategies (T-RETHROG: Talk, Reach, Throw, Row, Go); rescue priorities (people, kayak, paddle, gear); responsibilities of rescuer, group, and swimmers.

skills.name: Rescue & safety gear
skills.standard: Identify and demonstrate the use of appropriate rescue and safety gear for the kayak and planned activity.

skills.name: Wading risks
skills.standard: Understand the risks associated with standing and walking in these environments (including entanglement and environmental hazards).

skills.name: Swimming positions
skills.standard: Understand the pros and cons of different swimming positions.

skills.name: Swim with equipment
skills.standard: Self-rescue: swimming with equipment.

skills.name: Conserve energy
skills.standard: Strategies to conserve energy.

skills.name: Kayak types in rescues
skills.standard: Advantages and disadvantages during rescues of various kayaks (sit-ins, sit-on-tops, recreational kayaks).

skills.name: Wet exit
skills.standard: Calmly exit the kayak after capsize, using proper body position and contact with the kayak and paddle.

skills.name: Paddle recovery
skills.standard: Paddle recovery options.

skills.name: Kayak recovery
skills.standard: Strategies for kayak recovery.

skills.name: Swimmer tow options
skills.standard: Swimmer tow options.

skills.name: Re-entry / re-mount
skills.standard: Swimmer re-entry / re-mount techniques (stern entry, heel hook, rescue sling, paddle float).

skills.name: Bumping / bulldozing
skills.standard: Kayak bumping / bulldozing.

skills.name: Kayak towing
skills.standard: Kayak towing.

skills.name: Kayak-specific rescues
skills.standard: Kayak-specific rescues (boat over boat, boat to boat, scoop).

skills.name: Unresponsive paddler rescue
skills.standard: Unresponsive paddler rescue.

skills.name: Emptying water
skills.standard: Emptying water from kayak (on-shore, bailers, pumps).

skills.competency: The paddler can employ one technique for self-rescue and one for assisted rescue and then paddle to shore in Level 2 conditions. They can swim with their kayak to shore and move another person's kayak to shore.
skills.name: Capsize & Wet Exit
skills.standard: Performs a wet exit in two ways.
skills.exceedsStandard: Hang time exceeds 5 seconds, maintains control of kayak and paddle.
skills.belowStandard: Does not release the spray skirt or, exhibits evidence of panic, or performs exit before full capsize.
skills.l1Standard: Calmly exit the kayak after capsize, using proper body position and contact with the kayak and paddle.

skills.competency: The paddler can employ one technique for self-rescue and one for assisted rescue and then paddle to shore in Level 2 conditions. They can swim with their kayak to shore and move another person's kayak to shore.
skills.name: Assisted Rescues & Deep-Water Re-Entry
skills.standard: Performs one or more assisted rescues as rescuer and swimmer: Side-by-Side, Sling, or T-Rescue.
skills.exceedsStandard: For kayaks with at least a stern bulkhead, less than 60 seconds from initial contact to the swimmer sitting in a dry kayak.
skills.belowStandard: Too much water is left in the kayak to make it paddleable, or the paddler does not re-enter their kayak.
skills.l1Standard: Swimmer re-entry / re-mount techniques (stern entry, heel hook, rescue sling, paddle float).

skills.competency: The paddler can employ one technique for self-rescue and one for assisted rescue and then paddle to shore in Level 2 conditions. They can swim with their kayak to shore and move another person's kayak to shore.
skills.name: Self-Rescue
skills.standard: Performs one of: Scramble (cowboy), Paddle Float, or Re-enter and Roll (with or without paddle float).
skills.exceedsStandard: Can self-rescue using two or more methods.
skills.belowStandard: Is unable to self-rescue or is only successful in four or more attempts or takes more than 3 minutes.
skills.l1Standard: Swimmer re-entry / re-mount techniques (stern entry, heel hook, rescue sling, paddle float).

skills.competency: The paddler can employ one technique for self-rescue and one for assisted rescue and then paddle to shore in Level 2 conditions. They can swim with their kayak to shore and move another person's kayak to shore.
skills.name: Swim Rescue
skills.standard: Swims to shore (25 yards/meters) in full paddling gear and drains kayak.
skills.exceedsStandard: Swims to shore using a variety of techniques.
skills.belowStandard: Needs assistance to get to shore.
skills.l1Standard: Self-rescue: swimming with equipment.

skills.competency: The paddler can employ one technique for self-rescue and one for assisted rescue and then paddle to shore in Level 2 conditions. They can swim with their kayak to shore and move another person's kayak to shore.
skills.name: Move a Capsized Kayak to Shore
skills.standard: Moves a capsized kayak ~3 kayak lengths (~50 ft/15 m) via Bulldozing, Shove and Chase, or Contact Tow.
skills.exceedsStandard: Can employ multiple techniques to get the kayak to shore.
skills.belowStandard: Cannot get the kayak to shore in a reasonable time.
skills.l1Standard: Kayak bumping / bulldozing.

skills.competency: The paddler can employ one technique for self-rescue and one for assisted rescue and then paddle to shore in Level 2 conditions. They can swim with their kayak to shore and move another person's kayak to shore.
skills.name: Swimmer Tows
skills.standard: Performs swimmer rescue via a bow, toggle, or back deck swimmer carry.
skills.exceedsStandard: Can utilize their kayak to perform a swimmer rescue using a bow, stern, toggle, or back deck swimmer tow without hesitation and move the swimmer to a safe area using all the methods of swimmer tow.
skills.belowStandard: Cannot provide a safe and effective swimmer rescue or capsizes attempting to execute.
skills.l1Standard: Swimmer tow options.

skills.competency: The paddler can employ one technique for self-rescue and one for assisted rescue and then paddle to shore in Level 2 conditions. They can swim with their kayak to shore and move another person's kayak to shore.
skills.name: Scoop Rescue
skills.standard: Able to assist a swimmer into their kayak.

skills.competency: The paddler can employ one technique for self-rescue and one for assisted rescue and then paddle to shore in Level 2 conditions. They can swim with their kayak to shore and move another person's kayak to shore.
skills.name: Unresponsive Paddler Rescue
skills.standard: Able to bring a capsized paddler to the surface.

skills.competency: The paddler can employ one technique for self-rescue and one for assisted rescue and then paddle to shore in Level 2 conditions. They can swim with their kayak to shore and move another person's kayak to shore.
skills.name: Towing
skills.standard: Able to use towlines and various towing configurations to move a kayak.

skills.competency: The paddler can paddle safely, effectively, and efficiently in Level 2 currents while maintaining control of their kayak.
skills.name: Currents — Awareness
skills.standard: Demonstrates understanding of currents (tidal or river) in Level 2 venues.
skills.exceedsStandard: Has a thorough understanding of the specific challenges of paddling in current with L2 characteristics and can do so safely while leading a group or on their own. Demonstrates the ability to calculate the river flow rate, or tide and tidal drift stages, and incorporate results into a route plan.

skills.competency: The paddler can paddle safely, effectively, and efficiently in Level 2 currents while maintaining control of their kayak.
skills.name: Currents — Reading Water & Boat Control
skills.standard: Identifies direction of flow and eddies and paddles through currents in control.
skills.exceedsStandard: Maximizes drift for making progress, utilizes eddies to make progress against the flow, and enters and exits eddies under control.
skills.belowStandard: Cannot identify the direction of flow or consistently paddles against the flow.

skills.competency: The paddler can paddle safely, effectively, and efficiently in Level 2 currents while maintaining control of their kayak.
skills.name: Currents — Hazards
skills.standard: Identifies hazards and positions themselves to avoid hazards.
skills.exceedsStandard: Identifies paddling hazards in current and can inform others of such while leading the group in the safest route possible to avoid the hazards.
skills.belowStandard: Does not identify hazards in a timely fashion to avoid them.

skills.competency: The paddler can paddle safely, effectively, and efficiently in Level 2 currents while maintaining control of their kayak.
skills.name: Currents — Rescues
skills.standard: Performs a self-rescue, an assisted rescue, and contact towing in current.
skills.exceedsStandard: Effortlessly and unconsciously performs the “Core: Rescues and Towing” skills in current.
skills.belowStandard: Cannot perform “Core: Rescues and Towing” section skills in current.

skills.competency: The paddler can paddle safely, effectively, and efficiently in Level 2 wind and waves while maintaining control of their kayak.
skills.name: Wind/Waves — Awareness
skills.standard: Understands how onshore and offshore winds affect paddling conditions.
skills.exceedsStandard: Recognizes and understands the "why" behind increasing sea state (fetch, direction, duration, intensity) and how onshore and offshore winds affect paddling conditions and can paddle safely, effectively, and efficiently in such conditions.
skills.belowStandard: Does not recognize the increasing sea state or has difficulty paddling in the increasing conditions. Is unable to take the necessary action to protect from the increasing conditions.

skills.competency: The paddler can paddle safely, effectively, and efficiently in Level 2 wind and waves while maintaining control of their kayak.
skills.name: Wind/Waves — Tactics
skills.standard: Applies strategies for paddling at various angles to the direction of the wind.
skills.exceedsStandard: Understands the effects of paddling in the wind on the kayaker and how to work with the wind to increase paddling efficiency.

skills.competency: The paddler can paddle safely, effectively, and efficiently in Level 2 wind and waves while maintaining control of their kayak.
skills.name: Wind/Waves — Boat Control
skills.standard: Can control their direction and maintain stability in wind and waves.
skills.exceedsStandard: Unconsciously applies edging to maintain direction control before needing to make less efficient strokes to make direction corrections. Unconsciously maintains stability in wind and waves.

skills.competency: The paddler can paddle safely, effectively, and efficiently in Level 2 wind and waves while maintaining control of their kayak.
skills.name: Wind/Waves — Rescues
skills.standard: Performs a self-rescue, an assisted rescue, and contact towing in wind and waves.
skills.exceedsStandard: Applies numerous rescues and contact towing techniques and is effortlessly able to attain the “target” in wind and waves.

skills.name: Rocky — Awareness
skills.standard: Identifies hazards and landing areas on a map or chart.
skills.exceedsStandard: Has a thorough understanding of the additional challenges of paddling in rocky environments.

skills.name: Rocky — Tactics
skills.standard: Chooses appropriate landings and areas to play in the rocks.
skills.exceedsStandard: Chooses to paddle rocky shorelines and explore caves and arches with a firm understanding of risk vs. reward. Keeps the group in areas with a high level of reward and a low level of risk.

skills.name: Rocky — Boat Control
skills.standard: Maneuvers along rocky shorelines safely, effectively, and efficiently.
skills.exceedsStandard: Thoroughly understands water movement along rocky shorelines and can seamlessly link various strokes into maneuvers while working with the water movement.

skills.name: Rocky — Rescues
skills.standard: Performs a self-rescue, an assisted rescue, and contact towing close to rock features.
skills.exceedsStandard: Has a thorough understanding of the importance of performing rescues and the need to move to areas of less risk when paddling close to rock features. Can react unconsciously to apply an appropriate rescue or to move to a safer area to do so.
